fields.description:
# WS2 тАФ Overlay catalog + code-review prompts + move-catalog

## Why
The base reviewer (WS1) escalates to SPECIALIST overlays; the gate also triggers some overlays deterministically by file globs/content. This story authors that catalog (the specialist reviewers keyed by WS1's `OVERLAY_IDS`) plus the per-gate kernel plug-ins (verify preamble + Pass-4 code moves + the `threshold_for` posture resolver) the consumer seam requires.

## What
- The overlay catalog: one finder prompt per `OVERLAY_IDS` member (WS1 owns the enum; WS2 adds CONTENT), each a contract-bearing front-matter prompt with `applies_to` globs.
- A code-review verify-prompt preamble that EMBEDS `VERIFIER_RULES_SCAFFOLD` and re-grounds against the diff.
- A code Pass-4 move-catalog: a `MOVE_REGISTRY_SCHEMA` instance (each move `{name, template, applies_when}`), e.g. extract-helper, add-regression-test, threat-model, add-migration-guard.
- A code-review criteria registry (parallel to `plan_review/registry.py`) + a `threshold_for(criteria)` resolver + a DERIVED `criteria_routing.json`.
- The `code-review-coach` Pass-4 prompt (selects applicable moves; the kernel renders them).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-*.md` тАФ the overlay finder prompts + the verify preamble + the coach prompt.
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py` тАФ EXTEND WS1's module: add the criteria registry (per-criterion `exec`/`applies_to`/`default_posture`/`block_threshold`) + the `threshold_for` resolver, reading the derived routing index (mirrors `plan_review/registry.py`). WS1's `OVERLAY_IDS` enum already lives here.
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` тАФ the DERIVED routing index (per-criterion exec tier, `applies_to`, `default_posture`, `block_threshold`, `blocking_enabled`), the analog of `plan_review/criteria_routing.json`.
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/moves.py` тАФ the code move-catalog instance + its `code-review-coach` move-pick contract.
- OUT OF SCOPE: the escalation union + workflow wiring + `gates/code-review.yaml` (WS3); the secrets/security DETECTORS (WS5) тАФ this story authors the security overlay PROMPT, not the deterministic scanner.

## Threshold-loading mechanism (resolves the G6 BLOCK + the T1/T3/E1 "gates/code-review.yaml as config" advisories)
Thresholds follow the SHIPPED plan-review precedent EXACTLY тАФ they are NOT in any gate YAML:
- The per-criterion ROUTING (`exec` / `applies_to` / `default_posture` / `block_threshold` / `blocking_enabled`) lives in the DERIVED `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` тАФ the analog of `plan_review/criteria_routing.json` (and of `reviewers/index.json`). It is regenerated from the prompts' front-matter + the registry's defaults.
- The code-review criteria registry's `threshold_for(criteria) -> (block_threshold, blocking_enabled)` READS that routing index (exactly as `plan_review/registry.py` resolves `block_threshold`/`default_posture` from its `criteria_routing.json`). Default when a key is absent: `(DEFAULT_BLOCK_THRESHOLD=0.95, blocking_enabled=False)`.
- `gates/code-review.yaml` (WS3) does NOT hold threshold VALUES. It only gates WHICH criteria/overlays run, via `when: ${{ steps.triggers.outputs.<overlay> }}` тАФ exactly like plan-review.yaml's `include_<ID>`. The threshold-VALUE source is `criteria_routing.json` + the registry, full stop.

## Overlay catalog тАФ per-id `applies_to` globs (resolves the E2/T8 "vocabulary undefined" advisories)
WS2 authors one finder prompt per WS1 `OVERLAY_IDS` member, each with starter `applies_to` globs (and OPTIONAL content-keyword triggers handled by the existing `overlay_triggers` op, NOT a new mechanism). Starter globs (calibration deferred):
- `security` тЖТ `**/auth*`, `**/*secret*`, `**/security/**`, `**/signing*`, `**/*cred*`
- `performance` тЖТ `**/*.py` (gated to perf-relevant findings by the prompt; no narrow glob)
- `i18n` тЖТ `**/locale/**`, `**/*i18n*`, `**/translations/**`
- `a11y` тЖТ `**/*.html`, `**/*.jsx`, `**/*.tsx`, `**/templates/**`
- `db-migrations` тЖТ `**/migrations/**`, `**/alembic/**`, `**/*schema*.sql`
- `docs` тЖТ `**/*.md`, `docs/**`, `**/README*`
- `supply-chain` тЖТ `**/requirements*.txt`, `**/pyproject.toml`, `**/package*.json`, `**/*.lock`, `**/Pipfile*`
- `api-compat` тЖТ `**/*api*`, `**/schemas/**`, `**/*.proto`, `**/cli*`, `**/mcp_server*`
- `iac` тЖТ `**/*.tf`, `**/*.tfvars`, `**/k8s/**`, `**/helm/**`, `**/ansible/**`, `**/Dockerfile*`, `**/*.cdk.*`
- `tests` тЖТ `**/test_*.py`, `**/*_test.py`, `**/tests/**`
- `llm-prompts` тЖТ `**/reviewers/**`, `**/prompts*`, `**/*.prompt`, `**/schemas/*.schema.json`

These globs are authored in each overlay prompt's `applies_to` front-matter and SURFACED into `criteria_routing.json`; WS3's `overlay_triggers` op reads them to emit the deterministic Round-A `include_<overlay>` flags.

## Overlay prompt category + CI drift gate (resolves the G6/T8 drift-gate advisory)
The overlay + verify + coach prompts use `category: code-review-pass` (like WS1's base reviewer) тАФ NOT `category: review` тАФ so they stay OUT of the single-pass reviewer-selection catalog and the `reviewers/index.json` drift gate (which only indexes `is_reviewer` prompts). They resolve via `get_prompt(...)`. The DERIVED `criteria_routing.json` gets its OWN regenerate-no-diff drift check (parallel to plan-review's): a test regenerates it from the prompt front-matter + registry and asserts no diff.

## Coach prompt output contract + "deterministic render" (resolves the T8/F1 advisories)
- `code-review-coach` is a Pass-4 prompt that SELECTS among applicable code moves and emits move-picks `[{move_id, subject, finding_refs}]` тАФ it does NOT author prose. A registered structured-output contract (`code_review_coach`, in `code_review/contracts.py`) pins that shape (mirrors `plan_review`'s `_pass4_model` / `plan_review_coach` contract).
- "Deterministic render via the kernel" = the kernel's `coach(surviving, registry, *, pick, active_triggers)` renders each picked move's `template` deterministically (the `{subject}` substitution); the LLM only picks, the kernel renders. The move-catalog (`moves.py`) is validated at load via `validate_move_registry`.

## Cross-story contract тАФ `criteria_routing.json` + `threshold_for` (resolves the T7/T9/COH handoff advisories)
- `threshold_for(criteria: Sequence[str]) -> tuple[float, bool]` (block_threshold, blocking_enabled) тАФ the SAME `ThresholdResolver` shape the kernel `pass3_over_findings(..., threshold_for=...)` consumes.
- WS2 OWNS the resolver + ALL criterion keys, and SHIPS the secrets + High-Critical-security criterion keys with **`blocking_enabled=False`** placeholders (advisory in v1). WS5 later flips EXACTLY those two keys to `True`. (Reconciles the prior contradiction: WS2 ships them False; WS5 flips True тАФ neither ships a value the other silently assumes.)
- `criteria_routing.json` schema (per key): `{ "exec": "1-TURN"|"AGENT", "applies_to": [glob], "default_posture": "advisory"|"blocking", "block_threshold": float, "blocking_enabled": bool }`. Documented in the registry module docstring.

## Specifics pinned (plan-review remediation 2026-06)
- **`VERIFIER_RULES_SCAFFOLD` source:** `rebar.llm.review_kernel.VERIFIER_RULES_SCAFFOLD` тАФ embed its text verbatim in the code verify preamble (same as the plan-review verifier prompts).
- **Threshold defaults (concrete):** `threshold_for` returns `(0.95, False)` for every criterion EXCEPT the secrets/High-Critical-security keys (owned by WS5 to flip True). Calibration DEFERRED; values live in `criteria_routing.json`, not hard-coded and NOT in a gate YAML.
- **"resolve via the prompt library" observable:** `get_prompt(<overlay-id>)` resolves + the `criteria_routing.json` regenerate-no-diff drift check.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] One contract-bearing front-matter finder prompt per `OVERLAY_IDS` member, each with `applies_to` globs (the starter set above); all resolve via the prompt library.
- [ ] A code verify preamble embeds `VERIFIER_RULES_SCAFFOLD` (independence/atomicity/allow-insufficient/verdict-not-fix) and re-grounds against the diff.
- [ ] A code move-catalog (`moves.py`) validates against `MOVE_REGISTRY_SCHEMA` at load (`validate_move_registry`), each move carrying `applies_when` triggers.
- [ ] The code-review criteria registry exposes a `threshold_for(criteria) -> (block_threshold, blocking_enabled)` resolver that READS `criteria_routing.json` (mirrors `plan_review/registry.py`); thresholds start high тЖТ mostly advisory in v1.
- [ ] `criteria_routing.json` is delivered (the derived routing index: per-criterion exec tier, `applies_to`, `default_posture`, `block_threshold`, `blocking_enabled`) with a regenerate-no-diff drift test.
- [ ] `threshold_for` BEHAVIOR is tested (not just key existence): a default criterion тЖТ `(0.95, False)`; the secrets/security keys exist with `blocking_enabled=False`; an unknown criterion тЖТ the default.
- [ ] The Pass-4 coach prompt (`code-review-coach`) is authored with a registered move-pick output contract; a test asserts the kernel `coach()` renders a picked move's template deterministically.

## Plan-review remediation (Round-11, 2026-06-29)
- WS2тЖТWS5 threshold-resolver handoff made explicit: WS2 OWNS `threshold_for` + ALL criterion keys, shipping the secrets + High-Critical-security keys with `blocking_enabled=False`; WS5 flips exactly those two to `True`. Neither story ships values the other silently assumes set. The handoff is recorded in `criteria_routing.json` + asserted by a test (the keys exist with `blocking_enabled=False`).
